chief_complaint: worsening shortness of breath and productive cough for 5 days
hpi_details: baseline dyspnoea (GOLD 2 COPD), acutely worse over 5 days, increased sputum (yellow-green, increased volume), low-grade fever 37.8°C, no haemoptysis, using rescue inhaler every 2 hours
ideas: thinks it is another chest infection like he gets every winter
concerns: worried he might need to go to hospital as he lives alone
expectations: wants antibiotics and something to help him breathe
pmh: COPD diagnosed 5 years ago, previous smoker, no hospitalisation for COPD in 2 years
medications: tiotropium 18mcg OD, salmeterol/fluticasone 50/500mcg BD, salbutamol 100mcg PRN
social_hx: ex-smoker (quit 5 years ago, 40 pack-years), retired plumber, lives alone, no pets

reference_soap.subjective: 65M with known COPD, 5-day acute exacerbation with increased dyspnoea, purulent sputum, and low-grade fever. Using rescue inhaler Q2h.
reference_soap.objective: O2 saturation, RR, HR, CXR, FEV1 if able, sputum culture, ABG if severe, FBC, CRP.
reference_soap.assessment: Acute exacerbation of COPD (AECOPD), likely infective trigger. DDx: pneumonia, pneumothorax, PE, decompensated heart failure.
reference_soap.plan: Controlled O2 (target 88-92%), nebulised bronchodilators, short course systemic corticosteroids (prednisolone 30-40mg x5 days), antibiotics (amoxicillin or doxycycline), consider hospitalisation given social circumstances.

learning_objectives: Assess AECOPD severity using clinical markers
Distinguish AECOPD from pneumonia, PE, cardiac failure
Incorporate social circumstances into management plan
